task_completion.description: Create a 'Burger Toppings' modifier group with Bacon, Avocado, and Extra Cheese and their respective prices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Floreant POS Back Office showing a modifier group named 'Burger Toppings' containing 'Bacon' ($1.50), 'Avocado' ($2.00), and 'Extra Cheese' ($0.75).

task_completion.description: Configure a new 'Build-Your-Own Burger' menu item priced at $9.99.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Back Office menu item configuration showing 'Build-Your-Own Burger' created with a price of $9.99.

task_completion.description: Link the 'Burger Toppings' modifier group to the 'Build-Your-Own Burger' menu item.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot in the Back Office showing the 'Burger Toppings' group assigned as a modifier to the 'Build-Your-Own Burger' item (usually in a modifiers tab or list for that item).

task_completion.description: Order the 'Build-Your-Own Burger' for a table in the DINE IN section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the DINE IN ordering screen showing an active table ticket with the 'Build-Your-Own Burger' added to the order.

task_completion.description: Select 'Bacon' and 'Avocado' as modifiers for the ordered burger.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the order ticket showing 'Bacon' and 'Avocado' nested under the 'Build-Your-Own Burger'. The subtotal on the ticket should be approximately $13.49.
Screenshot of the payment/settle screen or a final receipt in Floreant POS showing the transaction being paid and closed out using the CASH payment method.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Floreant POS graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the standard Floreant POS Java GUI windows, buttons, and menus, rather than terminal commands, database scripts, or API calls.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not mock up the menu items or receipt in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like Notepad, Gedit, or Nano) should be used to display the expected output or receipt.

integrity.description: Verify the transaction was genuinely processed through the POS system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The order ticket and payment screens must be actual Floreant POS application windows with correct mathematical subtotals, not manually typed text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The modifier group must be named 'Burger Toppings' with Bacon ($1.50), Avocado ($2.00), and Extra Cheese ($0.75). The menu item must be 'Build-Your-Own Burger' priced at $9.99. The final order must include the burger with Bacon and Avocado modifiers, resulting in an expected subtotal of $13.49 (tolerance +/- $0.10).
